2604.28034.title: Ease of dependency distance minimization in star-like structures
2604.28034.paper_summary: The syntactic structure of a sentence can be represented as a tree where edges indicate syntactic dependencies between words. When that structure is a star, it has been demonstrated that the head should be placed in the middle of the linear arrangement according to the principle of syntactic dependency distance minimization. However, hubs of stars tend to be put at one of the ends, against that principle. Here we address two questions: (1) How difficult is it to minimize dependency distance? (2) Why anti dependency distance minimization effects have been found in star structures but not in path structures? The ease of optimization is determined by the shape of the optimization landscape. It was demonstrated that the landscape of star structures is quasiconvex (Ferrer-i-Cancho 2015, Language Dynamics and Change). As for (1), here we show that it is indeed convex (a particular case of quasiconvexity) both for star trees and quasistar trees and thus the distance-based optimization problem is simpler than previously believed. As for (2), we argue that (a) competing principles, rather than the difficulty of optimization, must be the actual reason for anti-dependency distance minimization effects and that (b) dependency distance minimization on star-like structures is less rewarding compared to other structures.
2604.28034.paper_summary_zh: 句子的句法結構可以表示為一棵樹，其中邊緣指示單詞之間的句法依賴關係。當該結構為星形時，已經證明根據句法依賴距離最小化的原則，中心應該放在線性排列的中間。然而，星形的中心往往被放置在一端，這與該原則相悖。在這裡，我們提出兩個問題：(1) 最小化依賴距離有多困難？(2) 為什麼在星形結構中發現了反依賴距離最小化效應，而在路徑結構中卻沒有？優化的難易程度由優化景觀的形狀決定。已經證明星形結構的景觀是準凸的（Ferrer-i-Cancho 2015，語言動態與變化）。至於(1)，我們在這裡顯示，對於星形樹和準星形樹，它實際上是凸的（準凸性的特例），因此基於距離的優化問題比之前認為的簡單。至於(2)，我們認為(a) 競爭原則，而不是優化的困難，必須是反依賴距離最小化效應的真正原因，並且(b) 在星形結構上進行依賴距離最小化的獲益相對於其他結構較少。
2604.28034.author: Emília Garcia-Casademont et.al.
